page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 32 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled public IPv4 addresses, and it operates without hardware GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of tested systems in stress-ng and Passmark CPU evaluations. Cryptographic capabilities are supported by hardware-accelerated AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction sets, which is reflected in strong Passmark CPU encryption and database operation scores. Memory performance is mixed; cached small-block read bandwidth is strong, whereas large-block uncached read and write bandwidths fall into the average tier.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the n2d-highcpu-64 is its low memory-to-vCPU ratio. While it provides high compute density for parallel processing, workloads requiring large in-memory datasets may experience bottlenecks due to the 1 GB per vCPU limit. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high density of dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources relative to its memory footprint, making it a viable option for CPU-bound tasks that do not demand extensive RAM.

This server configuration is suited for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, multi-threaded decompression, ray tracing, and image processing. It is also capable of handling Redis SET operations and text generation for small language models, provided the model footprint fits comfortably within the 64 GB memory boundary.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor, featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores and 64.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data shows strong performance in multi-core CPU execution, cryptography, and database operations, alongside average large-block memory bandwidth. Hardware-accelerated instruction sets such as AVX, AES, and SHA_NI support its cryptographic and mathematical capabilities. While cached small-block memory read bandwidth is strong, uncached large-block read and write speeds remain average. The instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-dense workloads that do not require large memory footprints, making it suited for parallel processing tasks, software compilation, ray tracing, and image processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 server with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM. It offers strong multi-core compute performance but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-64 features 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core compute performance.
tagline: A compute-dense x86_64 platform featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and a streamlined one-gigabyte-per-core memory ratio for parallel processing.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
64.0 GB of system memory providing 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU
Strong multi-core CPU, cryptographic, and database benchmark performance
No local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration included
Suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and parallel compute tasks
